_comment: Copy to settings.json and customize. Non-secret app settings only. Secrets live in .env. Paths may be absolute or relative to the project root.
_ai_plan_comment: How Claude usage is paid for. auth_mode 'subscription' (default) = your Claude Pro/Max login covers usage at the plan's flat rate; recommended model: Opus. auth_mode 'api_key' = pay-as-you-go on your own Anthropic API key (stored in .env as ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, reaches only the spawned claude.exe); recommended model: Sonnet. api_budget_monthly_usd is the pay-as-you-go monthly ceiling — Jarvis pauses (never overspends) when the month's metered cost reaches it; 0 disables the ceiling. Change all of this from Settings -> AI plan in the app.
_capability_tier_comment: How much Jarvis may do on its own. 'safe' (default) = proposes changes, you approve. 'powerful' = auto-applies non-destructive changes across your vault (destructive still confirmed), connectors on. 'unrestricted' = full power: self-edit, shell, write across vault+app, auto-approve everything. Backup-before-write, the audit log, and secret-file protection stay ON at EVERY tier (full power stays recoverable). Leave blank to hand-tune the granular agent_safety/permissions blocks instead.
_vault_path_comment: Default = the bundled Jarvis brain (de-personalized OS that ships with the app). Point elsewhere only to run Jarvis against a different folder of your own files.
_session_sync_comment: Cross-device chat continuity: the server keeps one copy of your chats + transcripts so every signed-in device shows the same list. Set false to keep chats only on each device.
_agent_safety_comment: Phase 3 - controls how Claude Code itself may act. draft_only (default, safe) = Claude gets no file-write/shell tools and runs in a throwaway workspace, not your files; it proposes changes the server applies after approval. legacy_direct = old full-agent behavior, PERSONAL USE ONLY, unsafe for a product. allow_code_mode (default false) = opt-in Claude Code mode chats: long-pressing the Operator Mode button escalates THAT ONE chat to raw Claude Code - full tools, direct edits and shell, no approval gate - while every other chat keeps the posture above. Owner-grade power; leave false unless this is your own machine and you explicitly want it.
_code_mode_comment: code_mode_dirs: extra folders a Claude Code mode chat can reach with file tools, on top of the vault (e.g. a repo you code on). code_claude_timeout_seconds: how long one code-mode turn may run before it is killed (default 3600 = 60 min; normal chats keep async_claude_timeout_seconds). Both only matter when allow_code_mode is true.
_integrations_comment: Optional add-ons, all OFF by default; the core product never needs them. google_calendar talks to YOUR own Apps Script bridge (calendar_bridge.gs) running in YOUR Google account. The bridge token is a secret and lives in .env as GOOGLE_CALENDAR_TOKEN, never here. Reads are direct; create/edit go through approval; delete is unsupported.
_permissions_comment: Level 3 controlled-action policy. Safe defaults: read the vault, write only to data/outputs + data/drafts, shell off, destructive off, writes need approval, back up before overwrite, audit everything. Widen deliberately.
